sourceMovie.title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls (1995) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Steve Oedekerk's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Madagascar
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls clicked for you, Madagascar (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls?
faq.answer: Try Madagascar, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa, Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted—each shares DNA with Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls?
faq.answer: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls (1995) is best described as Crime, Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ace Ventura Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
